items.title: Police investigating suspicious death of woman in New Town

children.content: Tasmania Police say the death of a woman in her 60s is being treated as suspicious. A man in his 50s has been arrested and remains in police custody.

image.altText: Three police officers stand under a small shelter in a suburban street

items.title: Tasmania to close legal loophole to help child sex abuse survivors

children.content: Tasmania will introduce laws to close a loophole which allows some organisations to avoid being liable for abuse perpetrated by individuals who aren't formal employees.

image.altText: Man in black suit stands for portrait in park in Autumn

items.title: The darkness of a Tasmanian town's night sky could be protected

children.content: The northern Tasmanian town of Ross is working to protect its night sky, a years-long process that would see it formally recognised for its darkness and clarity.

gallery.altText: A church at night with a starry sky in the background.

thumbnail.altText: A church at night with a starry sky in the background.

items.title: Dance helped stroke survivor Laura find confidence in her new body

children.content: As the social and health benefits of a not-for-profit dance group for over 50s are felt, industry insiders hope more funding will be invested in the arts sector ahead of the Tasmanian state budget.

image.altText: Older women doing dance practise in a studio with blue light

items.title: Spirit of Tasmania operator to get $506m government bailout amid bungled ferry rollout

children.content: The announcement comes as TT-Line continues to wrestle with the financial fallout from its bungled delivery of the new Spirit of Tasmania vessels.

image.altText: A large white and red ship docked in Hobart.

items.title: Racehorse shelters mandatory in sweeping Tasmanian welfare changes

children.content: Adequate shelters with a minimum floor size and at least one fixed wall to protect horses from wind and rain are new enforceable requirements for those keeping racing horses in Tasmania, as the state makes sweeping changes to animal welfare standards.

gallery.altText: Horses wearing coats in a paddock.

thumbnail.altText: Horses wearing coats in a paddock.

avatar.alt: A woman smiling at the camera.

items.title: TassieCast: Is Tasmania facing a cut to arts?

children.content: The state government has already chopped a number of creative courses from TasTAFE, leading to fears in the arts industry of a state-wide creative brain drain. And as the state budget looms, there are more worries. Airlie Ward chats with state political reporter Josh Duggan.

items.title: First home buyers 'up and about' at auctions after negative gearing changes

children.content: The government says the changes are aimed at levelling the playing field for young buyers bidding against property investors, while the opposition is pledging to undo the reforms, citing their potential to raise rents.

image.altText: A row of houses on a suburban street in Melbourne, in what looks like a colder month.

items.title: AI psychosis is on the rise and experts are sounding the alarm

children.content: Rodrigues thought an AI chatbot could help him escape poverty and build wealth. Instead, he became trapped in a spiral of delusion and dependence.

gallery.altText: Robot hand and human hand touch fingers echoing Michaelangelo's painting Creation of Adam

thumbnail.altText: Robot hand and human hand touch fingers echoing Michaelangelo's painting Creation of Adam

items.title: China quietly turns off supply of world's most used industrial chemical

children.content: Amid disruption from the Iran war, China has banned exports of sulphuric acid, a crucial component used to treat drinking water, refine petroleum and make batteries, clothing and phosphate fertilisers.

image.altText: beakers of coloured and clear liquid on a metal work bench both have glass measuring instruments in them one is being picked up

items.title: Taiwan remains committed to status quo after Trump's China trip

children.content: The Taiwanese government responds after US President Donald Trump's trip to China and his call for the two countries to "cool down".

image.altText: Trump and Xi, both wearing suits, look at something out of frame to the left

items.title: 'You're malnourished; you are overtrained': Olympic gymnast's warning

children.content: Alexandra Kiroi-Bogatyreva trained up to 10 hours a day, six days a week, often without the proper nutrition. She wants others to know what that can do to the body.

gallery.altText: A gymnast does the splits while jumping and holding a ball.

thumbnail.altText: A gymnast does the splits while jumping and holding a ball.

items.title: Divers who faced jail for cultural fishing lead sea urchin recovery

children.content: After decades of fighting for their traditional fishing rights, Aboriginal custodians are now fighting to save their sea country from ecological collapse.

image.altText: Two Aboriginal men stand in diving gear on rocks by the sea, holding their catch in net bags

items.title: Top Hamas military leader killed in Israeli air strikes, official confirms

children.content: Izz al-Din al-Haddad is the most senior Hamas official to be killed by Israel since a US-backed ceasefire agreement meant to halt fighting was implemented in October.

image.altText: The shell of a car on fire with people crowding around.

items.title: Love, lust and the NDIS: what intimacy looks like with a disability

children.content: It's a stormy February night in Adelaide, and Jacqueline Tedmanson is at the hospital with her partner Jackson, waiting to meet their first baby.

gallery.altText: A female burlesque performer poses confidently in a wheelchair. 5 months pregnant, she is in a rehearsal room.

thumbnail.altText: A female burlesque performer poses confidently in a wheelchair. 5 months pregnant, she is in a rehearsal room.

items.title: Sam Kerr bids emotional Chelsea farewell with record-equalling goal

children.content: Sam Kerr ties the record for the most Women's Super League goals for Chelsea as she plays her final match with the London club after more than six years.

image.altText: Millie Bright hugs Sam Kerr from behind on the field during a post-match presentation with Chelsea.

items.title: Push to make rental properties ready for portable power future

children.content: A standard power plug for charging your home from an EV is among the measures proposed to improve energy standards for rental properties.

image.altText: Robin wears a blue poloo shirt and stands in his garage, holding an EV charger.

items.title: Artist appointed, axed then reinstated from Venice Biennale triumphs

children.content: Last year, Khaled Sabsabi was appointed, sacked and then reinstated as Australia's representative to the Venice Biennale. Now he opens two installations at the world's most prestigious art exhibition.

gallery.altText: Khaled Sabsabi, a middle-aged Middle Eastern man, hair tied back stands in front of an intimately stitched green tapestry.

thumbnail.altText: Khaled Sabsabi, a middle-aged Middle Eastern man, hair tied back stands in front of an intimately stitched green tapestry.

items.title: Rochelle Jordan's new album is so good, even she listens to it

children.content: The Canadian singer won widespread acclaim with her third album. Now she brings it to Australia.

image.altText: Close-up black-and-white portrait of Rochelle Jordan in a light shirt and hoop earrings.

items.title: Test your homophones and your Gen Alpha slang

children.content: Are you ready to test your spelling skills like your little lunch depends on it? Come on in.

image.altText: Guy Montgomery beams as he stands in front of the Guy Mont set in front of a podium bearing a cup, purse and bucket.

items.title: First Nations art collective calls for industry inquiry to protect artists

children.content: APY Art Centre Collective members say a voluntary code of conduct isn't protecting artists from financial exploitation and misconduct in the industry.

image.altText: The exterior of a building with corrugated metal sheets and a bright red logo, reading APY Art Centre Collective

tags.title: Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Art

items.title: From cask to car, could red wine be Australia's next fuel?

children.content: Australia's wine industry is investigating whether its current 263 million litre glut of wine would be better used as biofuel to power cars, trucks or even aircraft.

image.altText: A posed photo of someone supposedly pouring wine into the petrol inlet of a white car.

items.title: You could hear a pin drop when Rob revealed why he fought a fire tax

children.content: Every time farmer and volunteer firefighter Rob Armstrong gets a call to battle a bushfire, something inside says not to do it. But he goes anyway. He knows what it’s like to be trapped in the flames and rescued by volunteers.

gallery.altText: A man wearing a navy blue long-sleeved shirt and brimmed hat with arms crossed. Behind imagery shows firefighters and bushfire

thumbnail.altText: A man wearing a navy blue long-sleeved shirt and brimmed hat with arms crossed. Behind imagery shows firefighters and bushfire

items.title: Former UK health minister says he will stand in any contest to replace Starmer

children.content: Former health secretary Wes Streeting, who resigned from cabinet on Thursday in preparation for the challenge, says his party needs a "proper contest" with the best candidates on the field.

image.altText: A young man speaks passionately at a conference.

items.title: Eight dead and 35 injured after train collides with public bus in Bangkok

children.content: Footage of the incident shows a group of vehicles queuing across a railway line, before a cargo train suddenly ploughs into the side of the bus and pushes it into oncoming traffic.

image.altText: A still from CCTV footage shows a train pushing a flaming bus through a crowd of cars.

items.title: Major highways left in the dark as copper theft surges in Queensland

children.content: The practice is costing Queensland ratepayers millions of dollars in repairs, and leading to widespread outages to lighting, communications and community sporting facilities.

image.altText: Side angle of a section of highway with four cars visible. The motion blur scatters the light coming from each headlight.

items.title: Who was still watching Harvey Weinstein almost a decade on from #MeToo?

children.content: Almost a decade after the #MeToo movement began, disgraced producer Harvey Weinstein's latest trial took place in a new climate. But supporters says its significance remains.

image.altText: A close up of Harvey Weinstein's face.

items.title: How this 100-year-old is 'enjoying life' by competing in athletics

children.content: Former ABC journalist and television executive Allan Martin details his renewed zest for life thanks to competing in athletics — all at the age of 100.

gallery.altText: Old man smiling in running gear with blue running track
A black and white archive photo from the late 1960s showing a male producer and male presenter on a TV set.

thumbnail.altText: Old man smiling in running gear with blue running track
A black and white archive photo from the late 1960s showing a male producer and male presenter on a TV set.

items.title: 'Don't be a d**k, Dezi!': New claims about Freeman police shooting

children.content: The owner of the Porepunkah property where Dezi Freeman shot two police officers says he rang and spoke to Freeman soon after the shooting.

image.altText: A man stands against a rural property

items.title: A hearty slow-cooked beef stew that will warm you up as the weather cools

children.content: Just 15 minutes of prep means your slow cooker will have a rich beef stew waiting at dinner time.

gallery.altText: A dinner bowl filled with rice and beef stew sits on a bench.

thumbnail.altText: A dinner bowl filled with rice and beef stew sits on a bench.

items.title: Taylor vows to alter Sex Discrimination Act following 'Tickle v Giggle' ruling

children.content: Opposition Leader Angus Taylor has vowed to amend the Sex Discrimination Act after the Federal Court upheld a landmark ruling that the exclusion of a transgender woman from a female-only app was discriminatory.

image.altText: Angus Taylor

items.title: After her husband's death, Annie discovered he had placed 100 bets a day

children.content: Annie Ashton's husband took his own life after suffering from a gambling addiction. Since then, she's campaigned for stronger regulation of the gambling industry and greater awareness of gambling harm.

image.altText: A close up of a woman starring off to the left with a serious look on her face

items.title: How Narendra Modi is turning India into a one-party nation

children.content: How Prime Minister Narendra Modi is turning India into a one-party nation.

gallery.altText: Smiling Modi in white kurta and mustard scarf waves as confetti falls around, nighttime, surrounded by security.

thumbnail.altText: Smiling Modi in white kurta and mustard scarf waves as confetti falls around, nighttime, surrounded by security.

items.title: Labor on track to win by-election in Brisbane seat of Stafford

children.content: At the end of counting on Saturday night, there had been a 4.1 per cent swing against Labor after preferences, with additional postal votes still to be counted.

image.altText: Luke Richmond in a park in Stafford

items.title: Trump says the US and Nigeria have killed second-in-command ISIS leader

children.content: US President Donald Trump says American and Nigerian forces have killed the second-in-command leader of ISIS.

image.altText: President Donald Trump talks to reporters before he boards Air Force One on Saturday.
